prerequisites: A Kisi account with organization administrator or organization owner rights (required to create an API key).
A Kisi API key generated from your account, supplied to the connector's required `api_key` field.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Kisi API key. The connector's required `api_key` config field holds this key, which is sent on each request in the HTTP header `Authorization: KISI-LOGIN <API_KEY>`. Requests also require `Content-Type: application/json` and `Accept: application/json`.

setupSteps.title: Sign in and open your account settings
setupSteps.body: Sign into the Kisi web dashboard. Click your name in the top right corner and select My Account. You need organization administrator or organization owner rights to create an API key.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: Open the API tab and click Add API Key. Enter a name for the key, provide your Kisi password, and click Add. Copy the displayed key immediately, since it is shown only once. Note that keys expire after 6 months of inactivity by default.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied key into the connector's required `api_key` field. The connector sends it as `Authorization: KISI-LOGIN <API_KEY>` to authenticate against the Kisi API.
